mission.title: Install and Configure Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE)
mission.description: Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. It provides seamless integration with Oracle Cloud services and offers features like automatic scaling and load balancing. Key trade-offs include reliance on Oracle's infrastructure and potential vendor lock-in.

steps.title: Install the Oracle Cloud CLI
steps.description:
The Oracle Cloud CLI is required to interact with Oracle Cloud services. This step installs the CLI on your local machine.

```bash

curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cli/master/scripts/install/install.sh | bash

```

> This command downloads and installs the Oracle Cloud CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster in Oracle Cloud with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-k8s-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.0 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

```

> Replace <your_compartment_id> and <your_vcn_id> with your actual compartment and VCN OCIDs.
commands: oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-k8s-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.0 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --file $HOME/.kube/config --region <your_region>

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> and <your_region> with your actual cluster ID and region.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step includes setting up monitoring and autoscaling for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/deploy/oci-cloud-controller-manager.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command deploys the OCI Cloud Controller Manager.
> This command sets up monitoring for your cluster.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --force --wait-for-state TERMINATED

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> with your actual cluster ID.

uninstall.title: Remove Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This command helps to clean up any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs.

```bash

oci lb load-balancer delete --load-balancer-id <your_load_balancer_id> --force

```

> Replace <your_load_balancer_id> with your actual load balancer ID.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This step upgrades your cluster to the latest version.

```bash

oci ce cluster update --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --kubernetes-version v1.30.1

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> with your actual cluster ID.
> Verify that the nodes are updated and in 'Ready' state.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with the kubeconfig file or cluster certificate.

**Fix:**
Regenerate the kubeconfig file using the correct cluster ID and region.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in default namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are no resources deployed in the default namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure that you have deployed applications or check the correct namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions to perform this action'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the user does not have the required permissions in Oracle Cloud.

**Fix:**
Check and update the IAM policies for the user or group.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create load balancer'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the subnets are not configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the subnet configuration and ensure they are in the same VCN.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new OCI features and improved integration with Oracle services.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.28.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.29, ensure to review the new IAM policies required for load balancer management.

resolution.summary: You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
